summary: Typosquat of chalk-template with install-time exfiltration via postinstall
details: Package name 'chalk-tempalte' is a single-character transposition typosquat of the popular 'chalk-template' package. The package ships a postinstall.js script that combines child_process execution with HTTP requests and system reconnaissance: postinstall.js line 5 requires child_process; lines 22, 48 perform GET requests; line 25 invokes http.request(); line 430 issues a POST. Multiple lines (20, 46, 287, 409, 427) reference 'hostname:' fields, consistent with collecting host identifiers and shipping them outbound. Execution is triggered automatically by npm's postinstall lifecycle hook on `npm install`, with no consent prompt. The combination of typosquat name targeting a top-tier package + automatic install-time execution + host enumeration + outbound HTTP POST is the canonical install-time exfiltration / dropper shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
